verses.text: Josiah is crowned at eight, begins to seek the God of David at sixteen, and at twenty starts purging Judah of its high places, groves, and images. His devotion grows year by year into action. A heart that seeks God early ripens into a life that cleanses the land.

Josiah is crowned at eight, begins to seek the God of David at sixteen, and at twenty starts purging Judah of its high places, groves, and images. His devotion grows year by year into action. A heart that seeks God early ripens into a life that cleanses the land.

Josiah is crowned at eight, begins to seek the God of David at sixteen, and at twenty starts purging Judah of its high places, groves, and images. His devotion grows year by year into action. A heart that seeks God early ripens into a life that cleanses the land.

He breaks down the altars of Baalim, smashes the images, and burns the bones of the idol-priests on their own altars, cleansing the land far and wide. His zeal is thorough and fearless. True reform does not tolerate idolatry but roots it out entirely.

He breaks down the altars of Baalim, smashes the images, and burns the bones of the idol-priests on their own altars, cleansing the land far and wide. His zeal is thorough and fearless. True reform does not tolerate idolatry but roots it out entirely.

In his eighteenth year Josiah sets about repairing the temple, gathering money and entrusting it to the workmen. He restores the neglected house of God. A reformer's care reaches the very fabric of worship, mending what former neglect let fall.

In his eighteenth year Josiah sets about repairing the temple, gathering money and entrusting it to the workmen. He restores the neglected house of God. A reformer's care reaches the very fabric of worship, mending what former neglect let fall.

The craftsmen and overseers do the work faithfully, the Levites diligent in every task. The repair proceeds in good order. God's house is honoured when those who labour on it work with honesty and care.

The craftsmen and overseers do the work faithfully, the Levites diligent in every task. The repair proceeds in good order. God's house is honoured when those who labour on it work with honesty and care.

As the money is brought out, Hilkiah finds the book of the law of the LORD, and Shaphan reads it before the king. The long-neglected word comes to light. When Scripture is recovered and read, God's voice is heard afresh in the midst of reform.

Hearing the law, Josiah rends his clothes and sends to inquire of the LORD, for 'great is the wrath... because our fathers have not kept the word.' The word pierces his heart. A tender conscience trembles at God's law and grieves over inherited disobedience.

Huldah the prophetess answers that evil will indeed come on this place, for the people forsook God and burned incense to other gods. The verdict is just and certain. God's patience has limits, and long-defied warnings ripen at last into judgment.

Yet because Josiah's heart was tender and he humbled himself and wept, God promises he will go to his grave in peace, not seeing the coming evil. Humility wins a personal mercy. God marks the contrite heart and spares it the worst, even amid national doom.

Josiah gathers all the people, great and small, and reads in their hearing all the words of the book found in the LORD's house. The king sets Scripture before the nation. Reform is shared when the whole people hear God's word for themselves.

Josiah gathers all the people, great and small, and reads in their hearing all the words of the book found in the LORD's house. The king sets Scripture before the nation. Reform is shared when the whole people hear God's word for themselves.

Josiah covenants to keep the LORD's commandments with all his heart, makes all present stand to it, and the people serve the LORD all his days. He binds the nation to God by covenant. A leader's wholehearted vow, owned by the people, holds a generation faithful.
